affordances.name: Familiar solution lock-in interrupt
affordances.mechanism: When a solution arrives suspiciously fast because prior experience supplies a familiar template, require the answer to interrupt fixed thinking and test whether the situation demands a different approach before commitment.

use_when: An experienced team keeps reaching for the same proven frame even though the situation feels structurally different.
The first solution arrived suspiciously fast and expertise may be blinding the group to alternatives.
The problem requires reframing, cross-domain analogies, or deliberate interruption of the dominant mental template before committing.
do_not_use_when: The familiar pattern has been tested against the current case and the assumptions still hold.
Avoiding fixation becomes a reason to reject all prior knowledge rather than distinguish useful pattern recognition from stale lock-in.
The answer would generate alternatives without testing whether the dominant template is actually wrong.
case_evidence_needed: The familiar solution or frame currently being applied.
The prior experience that makes it feel obvious.
The case features that may make the familiar template unreliable.
The alternative test, analogy, or frame that could break lock-in.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name the familiar template, identify what makes the case different, and specify the test or alternative frame that would reveal stale pattern lock-in.
evidence_required: The solution or pattern that arrived quickly.
The experience or template it comes from.
The mismatch between the template and the present case.
The alternative frame or interruption test.
good_output_shape: Identifies why speed may be a warning sign.
Distinguishes useful expertise from stale pattern lock-in.
Produces a bounded interruption test before commitment.

diagnostic_questions: What familiar solution is being reused here?
What about this case makes the familiar template potentially unreliable?
What small test or alternative frame would break stale solution lock-in?
misuse_guards: Do not reject prior knowledge merely because novelty is possible.
Do not generate alternatives for novelty theater without testing the dominant template.
Do not treat fast pattern recognition as reliable when the case structure differs.

source_evidence.source_quote: existing or previously learned solution prevents an individual from perceiving, or being open to, alternative solutions
an experienced team keeps reaching for the same proven frame even though the situation feels structurally different.
a problem requires reframing, cross-domain analogies, or deliberate interruption of the dominant mental template before committing.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 10 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports a familiar-solution lock-in interrupt that tests stale templates without rejecting expertise wholesale.
dropped_material: Did not extract learning-mode, word-search, or interrogative-self-talk material as separate affordances.
Kept Feynman and first-principles material as supporting context only.
open_questions: PR46 should test whether this card helps packets interrupt stale solution lock-in without producing novelty theater.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that avoiding fixation should not become a reason to reject helpful pattern recognition and prior knowledge.

source_evidence.source_quote: distinguishing helpful pattern recognition from stale pattern lock-in

absence_records.reason: The source warns that cognitive ease can make a familiar path feel true even when fluency is not a reliable test of fit.

source_evidence.source_quote: fluency is not easily distinguished from truth
getting stuck in solving a problem or understanding a concept as a result of becoming fixated on a flawed approach

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 10 source reading found strong reviewed depth for einstellung-effect as a familiar-solution lock-in interrupt.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether the card catches stale patterns without making prior experience look useless.
